jokes.text: The same cafeteria where Britta Perry publicly professed her love to Jeff Winger who then walked out on her. Unforgettable!
jokes.setup: Dean promoting a 'fresh start' dance for new beginnings
jokes.punchline: Immediately reminds everyone of the embarrassing incident they're trying to forget
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - the Dean's oblivious enthusiasm undermining his own message. Strong structure with immediate contradiction.

jokes.text: We're like Batman and Shaft. Batman actually had a guy. His name was Robin. Troy, Batman's friend didn't have to be Robin. It could have been Shaft, Dolemite... Leroy Brown. Don't let society limit your people.
jokes.setup: Troy makes an odd comparison between their friendship and Batman/Shaft
jokes.punchline: Abed's logical but ridiculous attempt to defend Troy's nonsensical analogy
jokes.explanation: Classic Troy/Abed dynamic - Troy's innocent absurdity met with Abed's overly logical support. Character-perfect.

jokes.text: Batman actually had a guy. His name was Robin. He rode around with him.
jokes.setup: Troy's Batman/Shaft comparison
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact correction with slight homoerotic undertone
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed literal-mindedness, subtly pointing out the subtext Troy missed

jokes.text: You're reading Troy's Twitter page where he posts everything Pierce says? 'Oldwhitemansays'? It's hilarious.
jokes.setup: Troy has been documenting Pierce's comments
jokes.punchline: The Twitter handle 'Oldwhitemansays' perfectly captures Pierce's identity
jokes.explanation: Excellent character integration - perfectly captures both Troy's social media innocence and Pierce's casual problematic statements.

jokes.text: How was your summer? Lived with you. Oh, yeah.
jokes.setup: Jeff asks Pierce about his summer
jokes.punchline: Pierce's matter-of-fact reminder that they live together, followed by awkward realization
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective awkward beat. Economy is perfect - maximum awkwardness in minimal words.

jokes.text: Hey, did you guys see Toy Story 3? - Yes! - Oh, I love Toy Story 3! - Hey! - Aah! - Hey! - Hey, Toy Story... Britta!
jokes.setup: Abed trying to change subject when Britta arrives
jokes.punchline: Everyone's awkward reactions and attempts to avoid the elephant in the room
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom awkward arrival beat - functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: I hope I'm not around when you do mean to be a tool.
jokes.setup: Jeff says 'I don't mean to be a tool' after being harsh
jokes.punchline: Britta's sharp comeback about his unintentional tool behavior
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback with good structure. Britta's wit shining through her embarrassment.

jokes.text: You are the...Coolest! Right. Um... At what, exactly? Being fearless! Honest. Speaking from your heart.
jokes.setup: Student calling Britta cool
jokes.punchline: Britta's confused reaction showing she doesn't understand her new fame
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Britta's discomfort with her accidental fame

jokes.text: 'Jeff Winger, I love you.' Right. Um... My heart may have overstated...
jokes.setup: Fan praising Britta for her fearless heart-speaking
jokes.punchline: Britta's uncomfortable admission that her heart exaggerated
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Britta's discomfort with her newfound fame and the reality behind her dramatic moment.

jokes.text: We technically frenched. I checked the make-out meter in this month's issue of... national review.
jokes.setup: Annie consulting a make-out meter in a magazine
jokes.punchline: The magazine is National Review (conservative political journal, not teen magazine)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - perfectly captures Annie's academic overachiever personality consulting the wrong type of publication for teen advice. Unexpected and character-specific.

jokes.text: Two credits into a music major Chang?
jokes.setup: Wondering what to call Chang now that he's a student
jokes.punchline: Highlighting Chang's minimal progress in his new academic pursuit
jokes.explanation: Solid character-based dig at Chang's new student status and lack of commitment.

jokes.text: Subject's talkin' my changuage.
jokes.setup: Chang explaining why he chose anthropology
jokes.punchline: Chang-ing 'language' to incorporate his name
jokes.explanation: Typical Chang wordplay - cheap pun but perfectly in character for his forced portmanteaus.

jokes.text: Star-burns has grown reliant on his star-shaped sideburns but still craves identity beyond them, so he added a hat.
jokes.setup: Question about Star-burns' new hat
jokes.punchline: Abed's overly analytical breakdown of Star-burns' identity crisis
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed - over-analyzing minor character details with psychological insight. Perfect character voice.

jokes.text: I'm hoping we can move away from the soapy relationshipy stuff and into bigger, fast-paced, self-contained escapades.
jokes.setup: Abed discussing improving their group dynamic
jokes.punchline: Directly addressing the show's format and his desire for concept episodes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment where Abed literally describes the show's creative direction and his role as genre-aware character.

jokes.text: What is it, exactly, that separates man from animal? Divorce?
jokes.setup: Professor's philosophical question about human uniqueness
jokes.punchline: Pierce's cynical and personal answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce response - cynical, personal, and delivered with complete confidence. Great economy.

jokes.text: Half a hat. Saves money. Thank you!
jokes.setup: Troy asking why Jewish people wear yarmulkes
jokes.punchline: Pierce's offensively reductive and wrong explanation, which Troy accepts
jokes.explanation: Character-perfect for both - Pierce's casual antisemitism and Troy's innocent acceptance. Uncomfortable but true to character.

jokes.text: 'Half a hat'? That is so offensive! I love 'oldwhitemansays'.
jokes.setup: Annie calling out Pierce's offensive comment
jokes.punchline: Immediately expressing love for the Twitter account that spreads his offensive comments
jokes.explanation: Sharp irony showing the disconnect between calling out bigotry and enjoying it as entertainment. Good structure.

jokes.text: Consider yourself Chang-ed.
jokes.setup: Chang paying to join their group
jokes.punchline: Chang-ing 'changed' to incorporate his name
jokes.explanation: Another forced Chang pun. Cheap wordplay but consistent with his character's verbal tics.

jokes.text: Looks like your food just walked out on ya. Same way you did Britta.
jokes.setup: Jeff's food literally leaving
jokes.punchline: Connecting it to his abandoning Britta at the dance
jokes.explanation: Clever parallel between literal and metaphorical abandonment. Good structure with nice callback integration.

jokes.text: I don't mean to sound like a tool, but maybe it's karma.
jokes.setup: Britta using Jeff's exact phrase from earlier
jokes.punchline: Throwing his own words back at him as revenge
jokes.explanation: Satisfying callback and character moment. Britta getting her revenge using Jeff's own words.

jokes.text: Annie, all women deserve to be with me. And vice versa. Granted.
jokes.setup: Annie suggesting he deserves someone specific
jokes.punchline: Jeff's narcissistic claim that all women deserve him (and he them)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff Winger narcissism delivered with complete sincerity. The 'granted' sells his total belief in this.

jokes.text: More of my urine for me.
jokes.setup: Professor offering students her urine tea
jokes.punchline: Her casual acceptance when they decline
jokes.explanation: Perfectly casual delivery of something deeply disturbing. Great economy and character establishment.

jokes.text: Britta... I love you. Now, I know this puts you in an awkwardly powerful position, but I have to know right now, in front of everyone... Do you love me too?
jokes.setup: Jeff's fake romantic declaration
jokes.punchline: The over-the-top public nature and manipulation of putting Britta on the spot
jokes.explanation: Good escalation moment. The public manipulation and fake sincerity create strong cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Shirley, would you consider spinning off with me? Just riffing, but we could open a hair salon together.
jokes.setup: Abed bored with the romantic drama
jokes.punchline: Literally proposing a sitcom spinoff to escape the current storyline
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke where Abed treats their real life like TV production, proposing an actual spinoff format. Highly original.

jokes.text: Well, I don't understand. Is this you being meta?
jokes.setup: Abed's spinoff proposal
jokes.punchline: Shirley directly acknowledging the show's meta nature
jokes.explanation: Great meta moment where character recognizes the show's own self-awareness

jokes.text: That's an ax. It's an ax?
jokes.setup: Shirley confidently identifying the cross as most important tool
jokes.punchline: Her confusion when told it's just an ax
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Shirley seeing religion everywhere, even in basic tools.

jokes.text: I'll show you the tool that's most important to our survival. But fair warning... it's my penis.
jokes.setup: Pierce promising to reveal the most important survival tool
jokes.punchline: It's his penis (with a fair warning beforehand)
jokes.explanation: Pure Pierce - inappropriate but delivered with complete sincerity. The 'fair warning' makes it funnier. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Thank the lord you're getting married! I was so worried about your souls ever since you had premarital sex on the table!
jokes.setup: Shirley's relief about the engagement
jokes.punchline: Revealing she knew about and was judging their hookup location
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley - religious concern mixed with TMI knowledge. Good callback integration and character voice.

jokes.text: Britta's marryin', Britta's marryin', Britta's marryin' Jeffrey Winger
jokes.setup: Wedding escalation getting out of hand
jokes.punchline: Abed hired an actual Irish folk singer for the fake engagement
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Abed's wedding episode concept. The specificity of hiring an Irish singer shows his commitment to the bit.

jokes.text: He's a George Clooney impersonator, but still, razzle dazzle, right?
jokes.setup: Abed introducing Jeff's 'best man'
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging it's fake but still proud of the production value
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed - prioritizing production values over authenticity. The 'razzle dazzle' captures his showbiz mindset.

jokes.text: Men are monsters who crave young flesh. The end! At least we have the decency to be ashamed of it and keep it a secret instead of publicly blurting, 'I love you,' to everyone who sleeps with us!
jokes.setup: Jeff defending his behavior with Annie
jokes.punchline: Extremely dark and misanthropic explanation of male nature
jokes.explanation: Dark but brilliant character moment. Jeff's cynicism taken to its logical extreme, revealing his self-loathing and philosophy.

jokes.text: You know, when there's three sprinkle donuts, you don't eat one and then lick another!
jokes.setup: Troy criticizing Jeff's behavior with women
jokes.punchline: Innocent donut metaphor for romantic/sexual conduct
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Troy metaphor - perfectly innocent yet perfectly apt. The specificity of 'sprinkle donuts' and the gross imagery makes it memorable.

jokes.text: Wait! I'm 'oldwhitemansays'? No wonder he says everything I'm thinking.
jokes.setup: Pierce discovering he's the subject of the Twitter account
jokes.punchline: His complete lack of self-awareness about his own thoughts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce moment showing his complete lack of self-awareness. The irony is beautifully constructed and character-perfect.

jokes.text: He thinks all dogs are boys and all cats are girls. There's no way to disprove that. Have you ever seen a cat penis?
jokes.setup: Troy revealing Pierce's bizarre belief about pet genders
jokes.punchline: Pierce's logical defense involving cat anatomy
jokes.explanation: Instant classic. Pierce's absurd belief defended with unassailable logic. The escalation to cat genitalia is perfect absurdist comedy.

jokes.text: You know, it's very 'season one.'
jokes.setup: Jeff criticizing Abed's TV/life confusion
jokes.punchline: Dismissing it as outdated like an old season of a show
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment where Jeff uses TV terminology to criticize Abed. The irony is that Jeff is becoming genre-aware himself.

jokes.text: TV makes sense and has structure, logic, rules. And likeable leading men. In life, we have this. We have you.
jokes.setup: Abed defending his preference for TV over life
jokes.punchline: Pointing out that Jeff is their unlikeable leading man
jokes.explanation: Devastating and brilliant. Abed perfectly articulates the show's central tension while delivering a perfect character burn to Jeff.

jokes.text: Gaaaa-yyyy! Ha. Gay fact.
jokes.setup: Jeff giving a heartfelt answer about respect
jokes.punchline: Chang's immature and homophobic dismissal
jokes.explanation: Cheap and offensive joke but true to Chang's character as the immature outsider trying to disrupt sincere moments.

jokes.text: I...Respect you! That's why you fail!
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to use respect to defend against physical attack
jokes.punchline: Professor's triumphant dismissal after beating him with weapons
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy payoff. The professor's point about respect vs. weapons proven through violence is darkly funny.

jokes.text: Did someone throw urine in my face? Oh. Sometimes I surprise myself under pressure.
jokes.setup: Jeff waking up confused about what happened
jokes.punchline: Shirley casually admitting to throwing urine
jokes.explanation: Great character moment for Shirley - surprising herself with her own capabilities under stress. Good contrast with her usual demeanor.

jokes.text: How 'bout oldwhitemansays... The TV show? Who would watch that?
jokes.setup: Pierce excited about monetizing his Twitter success
jokes.punchline: Troy's deadpan question about the audience for Pierce's thoughts
jokes.explanation: Good callback with meta-commentary on bad TV development. Troy's innocent question deflates Pierce's enthusiasm perfectly.

jokes.text: I was trying so hard to make our first week a great one, I didn't realize there was something great in front of us the whole time... an old woman drinking her own pee.
jokes.setup: Abed apologizing for forcing story concepts
jokes.punchline: Realizing the absurd professor was the real entertainment all along
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed meta-commentary on the episode's structure while acknowledging the absurdist comedy they overlooked.

jokes.text: But they're my friends. I'm your only friend! No. Hah hah hah! No! Ah hah hah hah! Noooooo!
jokes.setup: Chang's internal conflict about the study group
jokes.punchline: His descent into maniacal laughter and denial
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Chang's psychological break. Sets up his antagonist arc for the season.

jokes.text: ♪ 'cause boobies we gotta ♪ ♪ order is primate, family hominidae ♪ ♪ but genus is...♪ Homo. ♪ But you know you're into me ♪
jokes.setup: Educational song about human classification
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo woven into scientific taxonomy
jokes.explanation: Clever integration of educational content with comedy. The 'boobies' and 'homo' jokes are juvenile but work in context.
